Outline
Outline
Document tabs
 
 
 
 
Tab 1
0
 
Headings that you add to the document will appear here.
 
1. Introduction
2. Background and Literature Review
2.1 Fixed Distance Decay versus Dynamic Contextual Geofencing
2.2 Mathematical Optimization for Algorithmic Trade-offs
2.3 Dynamic Friction Analysis
2.4 Quick Summary of Past Research
2.5 The Research Gap
3 Methodology
3.1 Research Design
3.2 Data Sources and Collection
3.3 Analytical Framework and Tools
4 Results and Findings
4.1 Descriptive Statistic and Data Overview
4.2 Inferential Analysis and Hypothesis Testing
4.3 Advanced Analytics Findings
4.4 Channel and Campaign Performance
5. Analysis and Discussion
5.1 Interpretation of Core Findings
5.2 Analytical Depth: Causality, Moderators, and Mediators
5.3 Comparative and Benchmarking Analysis
5.4 Critical Evaluation of Analytical Methods
5.5 Strategic Implications of Findings
5B. Deeper Analytical Discussion
5.6 Data Quality, Bias, and Analytical Integrity
5.7 Customer Behaviour and Journey Analytics
5.8 Digital and Social Media Analytics
5.9 Marketing Mix and Budget Optimisation Analytics
6. Conclusion
6.1 Summary of Key Findings
6.2 Theoretical Contributions
6.3 Practical Contributions
6.4 Limitations of the Study
6.5 Directions for Future Research
6.6 Synthesis and Final Reflection
7. References
8.Appendices
Appendix A: Data Transformation and Feature Engineering Pipeline
Appendix B: Mathematical Optimization & Interaction Models
1. Geofencing Distance Decay with Non-Linear Contextual Adjustments
2. Gradient Boosted Decision Trees (GBDT) Loss Minimization
Appendix C: Supplementary Statistical Metrics and Model Diagnostics
